revision.text:
'''Yushima Tenman-gū''' Shrine (湯島天満宮) is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]] devoted to [[Tenjin]] the [[Kami]] of Learning. It is located in the [[Bunkyo]] ward of Tokyo, not far from the [[University of Tokyo]], and is a frequent site of prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams there in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[ema]]'' votives to petition the kami for success at exams.
Built in the year 458 for another kami, {{nihongo|''Ameno-tajikaraono-mikoto''|天手力雄命||}} of Japanese myth, the shrine was expanded in 1355 to enshrine the kami [[Tenjin]] as well. Currently both kami are enshrined at this temple. The was later rebuilt in 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]], and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Doshun]] and [[Arai Hakuseki]].
Due to Tenjin's frequent association with plum trees, Yushima Tenmangu maintains an extensive grove of plum trees, and holds a yearly festival called {{nihongo|''ume matsuri''|梅祭り||}} in February or March depending on when the trees bloom.
== Gallery ==
<Gallery> File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|Main shrine hall File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|Ema offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|Inari no Kami sub-shrine </Gallery>
== References ==
* {{cite web|title=Yushima Tenmangu Homepage| language=English| url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm| accessdate = 2010-04-21}}

revision.text:
{{Shinto}} '''Yushima Tenman-gū''' Shrine (湯島天満宮) is a [[Shinto]] shrine in [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]] devoted to [[Tenjin_(kami)|Tenjin]], the [[Kami]] of Learning. It is located in the [[Bunkyo,_Tokyo|Bunkyo]] ward of Tokyo, not far from the [[University of Tokyo]], and is a frequent site of prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams there in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema_(Shintō)|ema]]'' votives to petition the kami for success at exams.
Built in the year 458 for another kami, {{nihongo|''Ameno-tajikaraono-mikoto''|天手力雄命||}} of Japanese myth associated with strength and sports, the shrine was then expanded in 1355 to enshrine the kami [[Tenjin_(kami)|Tenjin]] as well. Currently both kami are enshrined at this temple. The shrine was later rebuilt in 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]], and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] and [[Arai Hakuseki]].
Due to Tenjin's frequent association with plum trees, Yushima Tenmangu maintains an extensive grove of plum trees, and holds a yearly festival called {{nihongo|''ume matsuri''|梅祭り||}} in February or March depending on when the trees bloom.

At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema_(Shintō)|ema]]'' votives to petition the kami for success at exams.
Built in the year 458 for another kami, {{nihongo|''Ameno-tajikaraono-mikoto''|天手力雄命||}} of Japanese myth associated with strength and sports, the shrine was then expanded in 1355 to enshrine the kami [[Tenjin_(kami)|Tenjin]] as well. Currently both kami are enshrined at this temple. The shrine was later rebuilt in 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]], and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] and [[Arai Hakuseki]].
Due to Tenjin's frequent association with plum trees, Yushima Tenmangu maintains an extensive grove of plum trees, and holds a yearly festival called {{nihongo|''ume matsuri''|梅祭り||}} in February or March depending on when the trees bloom.

Due to Tenjin's frequent association with plum trees, Yushima Tenmangu maintains an extensive grove of plum trees, and holds a yearly festival called {{nihongo|''ume matsuri''|梅祭り||}} in February or March depending on when the trees bloom. == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == References == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded = 458 | closed = | founder = | priest = | reisai = | honden = | address = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | phone = +81 3-3836-0753 | website = http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. It is devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[Kami]] of Learning. It is located in the [[Bunkyo, Tokyo|Bunkyo]] ward of Tokyo, not far from the [[University of Tokyo]], and is a frequent site of prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams there in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'' votives to petition the kami for success at exams. Built in 458 for another kami, {{nihongo|''Ameno-tajikaraono-mikoto''|天手力雄命||}} of Japanese myth associated with strength and sports, the shrine was then expanded in 1355 to enshrine the kami [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] as well. Currently both kami are enshrined at this temple. The shrine was later rebuilt in 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]], and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] and [[Arai Hakuseki]]. Due to Tenjin's frequent association with plum trees, Yushima Tenmangu maintains an extensive grove of plum trees, and holds a yearly festival called {{nihongo|''ume matsuri''|梅祭り||}} in February or March depending on when the trees bloom. == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == References == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | width = | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | dedication = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded = 458 | closed = | founder = | priest = | reisai = | honden = | address = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | phone = +81 3-3836-0753 | website = http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is a frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432-1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == References == {{reflist|2}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432-1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == References == {{reflist|2}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432-1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo Period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu - Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1st - 7th January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7th February - 7th March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25th May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1st - 23rd November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist|2}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | width = | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | dedication = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded = 458 | closed = | founder = | priest = | reisai = | honden = | address = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | phone = +81 3-3836-0753 | website = http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is a frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu - Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1st - 7th January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7th February - 7th March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25th May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1st - 23rd November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist|2}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]]

湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | width = | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | dedication = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded = 458 | closed = | founder = | priest = | reisai = | honden = | address = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | phone = +81 3-3836-0753 | website = http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is a frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. revision.comment: Hijiri88 moved page [[Yushima Tenman-gū]] to [[Yushima Tenmangū]]: See [[Wikipedia:Manual of Style/Japan-related articles#Temples and shrines]]. Also it's undoubtedly the more common spelling outside Wikipedia, although [[WP:COMMONNAME]] certainly doesn't apply.

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]

{{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is a frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]

{{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is a frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} * Hatsumode (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |date= |website= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 |quote=}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]

At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]

The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Tenjin faith]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}}[[Category:Beppo Shrines]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is a frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Tenjin faith]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]]
