The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:ファイル]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[Category:ファイル]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:ファイル]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} {{Authority control}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[category:Beppyo shrines]] [[category:Fu-sha]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}

{{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū}][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenman-gū]] | deity = [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]] ([[Sugawara no Michizane]])
[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]] | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, [[Bunkyō]], [[Tokyo]] [[〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in the [[Bunkyō]] [[Special wards of Tokyo|ward]] of [[Tokyo]], [[Japan]]. Established in 458, it is now devoted to [[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]], the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to [[Ueno Park]], and not far from the [[University of Tokyo]]. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''[[Ema (Shintō)|ema]]'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his [[Prunus mume|plum trees]] (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''[[Ame-no-Tajikarao|Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto]]''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''[[Tenjin (kami)|Tenjin]]''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[deification]] of [[Sugawara no Michizane]] (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the [[Heian period]]. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the [[Edo period]] when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as [[Hayashi Razan|Hayashi Doshun]] (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of [[Shinto architecture]].{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|[[Ema (Shintō)|Ema]] offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|[[Inari Okami|Inari]] no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * [[Hatsumōde]] (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of [[Yushima Station (Tokyo)|Yushima Station]] on the [[Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|Chiyoda Line]].{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, [[Nagano (city)]]]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of [[Nagano (city)]] next to the [[Kitano Museum of Art]]{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Yushima Tenman-gu}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Tenjin Shrines]] [[Category:Ueno, Tokyo]] [[Category:Sugawara no Michizane]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:Tokyo Metropolitan Designated Tangible Cultural Property]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū}][[Category:qq]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] [[Category:dd]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 7]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Yushima Tenmangū]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]]]] {{Tenjin Faith}}
