[[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[sn:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q335606]] Wikidata name: Yushima Tenman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}} ({{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}})
{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} [[:en:〒|〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō}} {{ill|Special wards of Tokyo|en|Special wards of Tokyo|WD=Q308891|lt=ward}} of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Established in 458, it is now devoted to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}, the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Park}}, and not far from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}}. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=ema}}'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his {{ill|Prunus mume|en|Prunus mume|WD=Q157763|lt=plum trees}} (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto}}''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''{{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[:en:Deification|Deification]] of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema}} offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|{{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari}} no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}} (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)}}]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]] {{Commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} [[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]] [[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[sn:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q335606]] Wikidata name: Yushima Tenman-gū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}} ({{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}})
{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} [[:en:〒|〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō}} {{ill|Special wards of Tokyo|en|Special wards of Tokyo|WD=Q308891|lt=ward}} of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Established in 458, it is now devoted to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}, the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Park}}, and not far from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}}. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=ema}}'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his {{ill|Prunus mume|en|Prunus mume|WD=Q157763|lt=plum trees}} (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto}}''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''{{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[:en:Deification|Deification]] of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)}}]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Tenjin revision.text:
[[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[sn:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}} ({{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}})
{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto}} | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} [[:en:〒|〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō}} {{ill|Special wards of Tokyo|en|Special wards of Tokyo|WD=Q308891|lt=ward}} of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. Established in 458, it is now devoted to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}, the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Park}}, and not far from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo}}. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=ema}}'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his {{ill|Prunus mume|en|Prunus mume|WD=Q157763|lt=plum trees}} (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto}}''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''{{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin}}''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[:en:Deification|Deification]] of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema}} offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|{{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari}} no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde}} (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)}}]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]] {{Commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} [[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]] [[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[sn:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}} ({{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}})
{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō|1=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} [[:en:〒|〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō|1=Bunkyō}} {{ill|Special wards of Tokyo|en|Special wards of Tokyo|WD=Q308891|lt=ward|1=Special wards of Tokyo}} of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Established in 458, it is now devoted to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}, the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Park|1=Ueno Park}}, and not far from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}}'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his {{ill|Prunus mume|en|Prunus mume|WD=Q157763|lt=plum trees|1=Prunus mume}} (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''{{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[:en:Deification|Deification]] of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun|1=Hayashi Razan}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture|1=Shinto architecture}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}} offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|{{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}} (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station|1=Yushima Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line|1=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}}]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art|1=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]] {{Commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} [[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]] [[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[ja:湯島天満宮]] [[ko:유시마 텐만구]] [[pl:Yushima Tenjin]] [[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun|1=Hayashi Razan}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art|1=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}} ({{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}})
{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō|1=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} [[:en:〒|〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō|1=Bunkyō}} {{ill|Special wards of Tokyo|en|Special wards of Tokyo|WD=Q308891|lt=ward|1=Special wards of Tokyo}} of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Established in 458, it is now devoted to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}, the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Park|1=Ueno Park}}, and not far from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}}'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his {{ill|Prunus mume|en|Prunus mume|WD=Q157763|lt=plum trees|1=Prunus mume}} (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''{{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[:en:Deification|Deification]] of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun|1=Hayashi Razan}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture|1=Shinto architecture}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}} offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|{{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}} (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station|1=Yushima Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line|1=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}}]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art|1=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]] {{Commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} [[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]] [[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[ja:湯島天満宮]] [[ko:유시마 텐만구]] [[pl:Yushima Tenjin]] [[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[co:commons:Category:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]][[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[en:Yushima Tenmangū]][[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]][[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[ja:湯島天満宮]][[ko:유시마 텐만구]][[pl:Yushima Tenjin]][[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]][[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Tokyo, Japan}} {{Use dmy dates|date=May 2018}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Yushima Tenman-gū
湯島天満宮 | image = Yushima Tenman-gū - Tokyo, Japan - DSC04975.JPG | caption = front view of the ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|42|28|N|139|46|6|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = [[Tenmangū|Tenman-gū]] | deity = {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}} ({{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}})
{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}} | founded_by = | established = 458 | date_destroyed = | location = 3 Chome-30-1 Yushima, {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō|1=Bunkyō}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}} [[:en:〒|〒]] 113-0034 | website = {{URL|http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Yushima Tenman-gū'''|湯島天満宮}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in the {{ill|Bunkyō|en|Bunkyō|WD=Q212713|lt=Bunkyō|1=Bunkyō}} {{ill|Special wards of Tokyo|en|Special wards of Tokyo|WD=Q308891|lt=ward|1=Special wards of Tokyo}} of {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo|1=Tokyo}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. Established in 458, it is now devoted to {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}, the [[kami]] of Learning. For this reason, it is also called '''Yushima Tenjin'''. It is located very close to {{ill|Ueno Park|en|Ueno Park|WD=Q746216|lt=Ueno Park|1=Ueno Park}}, and not far from the {{ill|University of Tokyo|en|University of Tokyo|WD=Q7842|lt=University of Tokyo|1=University of Tokyo}}. It is frequently sited by prospective students hoping to pass the entrance exams, particularly in April. At this time, the temple receives many offerings of ''{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}}'', votive tablets to petition the kami for success.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} One of the most famous{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }}{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} features of the shrine are the blossoms of his {{ill|Prunus mume|en|Prunus mume|WD=Q157763|lt=plum trees|1=Prunus mume}} (''ume'') in the spring. In February and March, the annual festival ''Ume [[Japanese festivals|Matsuri]]'' is held, attracting many visitors.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == History == ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' was originally established for the worship of {{nihongo|''{{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|en|Ame-no-Tajikarao|WD=Q849412|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-mikoto|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao}}''|天手力雄命||}},{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} a [[kami]] associated with sports and physical power found in [[Japanese mythology]] (most famously in the ''[[Kojiki]]'' and ''[[Nihon Shoki]]''). In February 1355 the shrine was expanded to enshrine the kami {{nihongo|''{{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|WD=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}''|天神||}} as well.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} ''Tenjin'' is the [[:en:Deification|Deification]] of {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} (845–903), a famous scholar, poet and politician from the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}. As a kami he is associated to scholarship and learning.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} Currently both kami are enshrined in ''Yushima Tenman-gū''. The shrine was later rebuilt in October 1455 at the behest of local warlord [[:en:Ota Dokan|Ota Dokan]] (1432–1486), and enjoyed greater popularity during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}} when it was visited by such Confucian scholars as {{ill|Hayashi Razan|en|Hayashi Razan|WD=Q558279|lt=Hayashi Doshun|1=Hayashi Razan}} (1583–1657) and [[Arai Hakuseki]] (1657–1725).{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The current structures of ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' were rebuilt in 1995.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} They were made using only cypress,{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} and following closely the tradition of {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto architecture|1=Shinto architecture}}.{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Gallery == File:Yushima tenmangu shrine.jpg|[[Honden|Main hall]] File:Yushima tenmangu ema.jpg|{{ill|Ema (Shinto)|en|Ema (Shinto)|WD=Q687288|lt=Ema|1=Ema (Shinto)}} offered at Yushima Tenmangu File:Yushima tenmangu inari.jpg|{{ill|Inari Ōkami|en|Inari Ōkami|WD=Q719665|lt=Inari|1=Inari Ōkami}} no Kami sub-shrine File:Reitaisai -Yushima 07.jpg|Reitaisai File:100 views edo 117.jpg|[[Hiroshige]] == Annual events == The yearly events and festival at ''Yushima Tenman-gū'' are:{{cite web |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/eng-fes.htm |title=Yushima Tenmangu – Festivals |author= |publisher=Yushima Tenman-gū |access-date=2017-12-26 }} * {{ill|Hatsumōde|en|Hatsumōde|WD=Q1032381|lt=Hatsumōde|1=Hatsumōde}} (New Year's visit) 1 – 7 January * Ume Matsuri (Plum trees festival) 7 February – 7 March * Tenjin-sai (The grand festival) 25 May * Kiku Matsuri (The chrysanthemums flowers festival) 1 – 23 November == Access == There is no admission fee for visitors to enter the shrine precincts. It is open from 6 am to 8 pm (the shop from 8:30 am to 7 pm).{{cite web |url=http://www.gotokyo.org/en/kanko/bunkyo/education/45.html |title=Yushima Tenmangu |author= |publisher=Official Tokyo Travel Guide |access-date=2017-12-26 }} The entrances is at a 2-minute walk from Exit 3 of {{ill|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|en|Yushima Station (Tokyo)|WD=Q390694|lt=Yushima Station|1=Yushima Station (Tokyo)}} on the {{ill|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|en|Tokyo Metro Chiyoda Line|WD=Q1075089|lt=Chiyoda Line|1=Chiyoda Line}}.{{cite web |url=https://www.japanvisitor.com/japan-temples-shrines/yushima-tenjin |title=Yushima Tenjin |author= |publisher=Japan Visitor |access-date=2017-12-26 }} == Branch == [[File:Yushimatennmangu Nagano.jpg|thumb|Yushimatennmangu Branch Shrine in Wakaho, {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}}]] There is a branch of Yushima Tenmangū located in the Wakahowatauchi district of {{ill|Nagano (city)|en|Nagano (city)|WD=Q128849|lt=Nagano (city)|1=Nagano (city)}} next to the {{ill|Kitano Museum of Art|en|Kitano Museum of Art|WD=Q63565237|lt=Kitano Museum of Art|1=Kitano Museum of Art}}{{cite web |title=湯島天神 |url=http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/shinano/shinano.htm |website=湯島天満宮信濃分社 |publisher=Yushima Tenmangu |access-date=25 May 2019}} == References == {{reflist}} == External links == {{commons Category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} *[http://www.yushimatenjin.or.jp/pc/eng-page/english.htm Yushima Tenman-gū Official website] {{Tenjin Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ [[en:Yushima Tenmangū]] [[ar:معبد يوشيما تينمان]] {{Commons category|Yushima Tenman-gū}} [[de:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[fa:معبد یوشیما-تنمان]] [[fr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[ja:湯島天満宮]] [[ko:유시마 텐만구]] [[pl:Yushima Tenjin]] [[tr:Yushima Tenman-gū]] [[zh:湯島天滿宮]] {{wikidata link|Q335606}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Bunkyō]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]]
